questions.text: The army stands at the ocean's edge; Lanka lies across the water and every eye turns to you for a way over.

options.label: Ideas pour out of you faster than they can be tried
You offer several clever approaches worth attempting
You put forward one workable plan
You wait to hear what the others devise
Your mind comes up empty; someone else must find the way

questions.text: A wandering sage describes a distant kingdom you have never seen.

options.label: You picture every spire, river and garden as if you walked there
You imagine it clearly, scene by scene
You form a rough picture of it
You can barely conjure an image
You see nothing; it stays mere words

questions.text: A guru lays out a subtle teaching on dharma, just once, and waits.

options.label: You grasp it at once and see further than was said
You understand it quickly and clearly
You get there after turning it over
You need it explained again
It stays beyond you however it is told

questions.text: Round the fire in the forest, the talk drifts to the soul, fate, and the meaning of it all.

options.label: Such talk wearies you; you'd rather tend to the camp
You have little patience for it, but you let it run
You'll join in if it turns to something useful
You find the questions worth turning over
You delight in chasing the deepest mysteries for their own sake

questions.text: You stand in a hermitage left in ruins and are asked how it might be restored.

options.label: You can't picture it; you only see the rubble
Imagining it does not come easily
You sketch a rough idea of it
You see it taking shape fairly clearly
A whole vision blooms before you, vivid in every detail

questions.text: At dusk the word comes: you must leave for fourteen years of exile at first light.

options.label: You are already ready; nothing catches you unequipped
You prepare thoroughly, with time to spare
You gather what you can in time
You scramble and forget a few things
You set out unprepared and hope for the best

questions.text: A treaty of alliance is read aloud before you set your seal to it.

options.label: You catch every clause and the smallest wording
You note the important details carefully
You follow the main points
You skim past most of it
You barely listen and simply agree

questions.text: The host must make camp on the shore for the long siege ahead.

options.label: Everything must have its place; you order the whole camp
You keep things tidy and arranged
You impose some order where it matters
You leave most of it loose
You let the camp fall into disarray; order is not your concern

questions.text: A delicate duty falls to you with no instructions and much at stake.

options.label: You'll make a mess of it before you make it right
Things tend to get tangled in your hands
It comes out fine, with a few loose ends
You handle it cleanly
You carry it out flawlessly, nothing left amiss

questions.text: A hard and thankless task is laid upon you to perform.

options.label: You slip away and leave it undone
You put it off as long as you can
You do it, grumbling, in the end
You see it through dutifully
You would never abandon a duty, whatever the cost

questions.text: A great victory feast fills the hall with music and light.

options.label: You are at the heart of it; the celebration revolves around you
You are lively and draw people in
You enjoy it without taking center stage
You keep to the quiet edges
You would rather not be there at all

questions.text: Envoys from an unfamiliar court arrive and stand waiting.

options.label: You step up first and open the talk
You readily strike up conversation
You speak once you are spoken to
You wait for others to begin
You avoid starting anything and hang back

questions.text: A whole assembly falls silent and turns to hear you address them.

options.label: You thrive with every eye upon you
You are comfortable holding their attention
You manage it, though you do not seek it
You feel the gaze and shrink a little
You dread it and wish another would speak

questions.text: A war council debates the next move, voices rising on every side.

options.label: You stay in the background and let it unfold
You mostly keep to the edges
You speak up now and then
You are right in the thick of it
You command the whole discussion from the front

questions.text: You are brought among a forest people you have never met.

options.label: You fall silent and keep to yourself among them
You stay reserved and speak little
You warm up slowly
You mix in without much trouble
You greet every stranger as warmly as an old friend

questions.text: A grieving widow comes before you and pours out her sorrow.

options.label: Her grief becomes your own; you feel it to the bone
You are deeply moved and offer real comfort
You acknowledge her pain
You stay rather detached
Her feelings do not reach you

questions.text: A weary stranger begs your help just as you set out on your own urgent errand.

options.label: You drop everything to help, however long it takes
You make real time for them
You help if it can be quick
You would rather not be delayed
You pass on by; your errand comes first

questions.text: A frightened messenger trembles before you, bearing ill news.

options.label: You calm him at once; he feels safe in your presence
You put him at ease
You hear him out plainly
You leave him on edge
Your manner only frightens him more

questions.text: A rival speaks foolishly and boastfully in open court.

options.label: You cut him down with a cruel, cutting word
You let a sharp jab slip
You answer firmly but fairly
You hold your tongue
You would never demean another, even a fool

questions.text: At a gathering, people around you share their lives and their troubles.

options.label: The affairs of others simply do not interest you
You pay them little mind
You listen politely enough
You are genuinely curious about them
You take a deep interest in everyone you meet

questions.text: On the eve of battle the carefully laid plan begins to unravel.

options.label: Panic grips you at once
You grow tense and rattled
You feel the strain but hold steady
You stay mostly calm
The pressure barely touches you

questions.text: Someone you love has ridden into danger, and the night stretches long.

options.label: You are consumed with worry, unable to rest
You fret over every possibility
You worry a while, then set it aside
You trust it will turn out well
You hardly worry at all

questions.text: Across a single trying day, fortunes rise and fall again and again.

options.label: Your mood lurches wildly from hour to hour
Your feelings swing fairly often
You have your ups and downs
You stay fairly even
Your temper holds steady through it all

questions.text: The realm is at peace and the day passes unhurried.

options.label: You are perfectly at ease, untroubled
You feel relaxed and calm
You are somewhat settled
An undercurrent of unease stays with you
You can never truly relax, even now

questions.text: On the long road the hard losses begin to mount.

options.label: You rarely feel low; you press straight on
You dip only briefly, then recover
You feel it a while before bouncing back
The gloom settles over you and lingers
It pulls you into a darkness hard to climb out of
